blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: How about my strategy? It worked perfectly, didn't it?
Screamer licked his lips.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: He never thought Aider was a good guy. Even if he was, would there be anyone in the graduating class who trusted others more than themselves?
However, strong self-assertion is often difficult for others to digest.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Anyway, enough. Reverse position? Were you trying to ruin the game? You should have at least told us in advance.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Hehe, sorry. It was my trump card, so I kept it hidden. Anyway, now that Maya is down, we have the advantage. I was actually planning to make them lose by disqualification.
Nade's interference had thwarted his plan.
Aider prided himself on being crazy, but Nade's face at that moment was something far beyond normal.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Aider.
At Shirone's voice, Aider turned around.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Shirone, this is our camp. Oh, does it not matter by the rules? Still, eavesdropping on our strategy is cowardly.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: ...Why did you do it?
Aider tilted his head. Then, as if realizing something, he snapped his fingers.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Oh, you mean Maya? Why? You didn't come all the way here to say something boring, did you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll ask again. Why did you do it?
Aider's expression twisted.
Now that his true nature was revealed, he no longer pretended to be intimidated by his seniors.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: What's the problem? I was just trying to put the guard in a groggy state. Screamer hit, Nade hit. Maya is just too weak. If it were Amy...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't misunderstand. I'm not here to get angry. That's not what I want to ask.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Really?
Aider clasped his hands behind his head.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I told you clearly after the survival test.
Shirone's eyes turned cold.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't mess with me.
Shirone's eyes turned cold.
He had said he would crush anyone who tried to mess with him. In other words, Aider was now on Shirone's hit list.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Hahaha! So, should I say, 'Oh, I understand. I won't mess with you again'?
In the past, he might have pretended to be scared, but now, with the power of Fermi's organization behind him, he had nothing to fear.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: As long as you understand. I just wanted to confirm that.
Shirone turned and walked back to his camp.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone turned and walked back to his camp.
His friends approached. With only a minute left until the start of the sixth round, they needed to quickly strategize.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, let's defend this round. Maya needs time to recover her mental strength.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: No, we'll end it here. I'll handle it, just block Screamer's team. If I'm alone as the forward, they won't come to back him up.
Maya wouldn't be able to recover during the match. Aider's violence had inflicted too deep a wound on her, who had strengthened her omnipotence by excluding her core.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Shirone, don't get excited. I'm annoyed too, but winning is important. Losing will make you even angrier. You know that, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine. Really, I'm okay.
Shirone smiled with his eyes, then suddenly turned with a cold expression.
The terrifying chill made his friends step aside.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: This is bad. He's really angry.
At this point, no one could stop Shirone.
As expected, as soon as the sixth round started, Shirone flew straight at Aider without looking at anything else.
Whew
Aider's smiling lips trembled. It was a strange expression, a mix of fear and excitement.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The one with the strongest mental strength in the graduating class was flying at him to crush him.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The more, the merrier.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The thought of not being able to touch the enemy he wanted to kill made his body tingle.
Aider dodged through the barrage of Photon Cannons, increasing Shirone's frustration.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Hahaha! What now? I haven't been hit once. It'll take a lifetime to pay back for hitting Maya.
Shirone didn't respond and landed in front of the flag.
Wind Cutters formed in Aider's hands. He planned to counterattack the moment Shirone grabbed the flag.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Ataraxia.
As a pale light formed 10 meters above the peak, drawing a huge circle, exclamations erupted from outside the battlefield.
It was twice the size of the halo he had used against Dante.
Aider stared blankly at the halo. Then, realizing something, he hurriedly retreated outside its range.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Damn. This guy is really annoying.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: There was indeed such a method. By deploying Ataraxia above the peak, even if someone grabbed the flag, they couldn't easily approach.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: In that case...
Aider crouched low, calculating the timing to jump out. Once the magic circle was complete, there would be no opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 30 seconds.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: 30 seconds left. If you can't stop me within that time, you'll never be able to beat me.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I know that, damn it.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Knowing it but being unable to do anything was because he had seen the power of Shirone's magic circle with his own eyes.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: If he got caught up in it, he might end up in the infirmary for the next round, regardless of winning or losing.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: He wouldn't kill me, right?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: After all, this was the 2000 system.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: In a real battle, he would never make such a choice, but with the filter of simulation, it seemed like a winnable gamble.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I'm going in!
The fact that a magician's power drops to less than half in a time-split state also contributed to Aider's decision.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: It should have been that way.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: But as the Photon Cannon brushed past, his hair stood on end.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Damn! Is it still impossible?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: In terms of single combat power, Shirone was expected to be among the top in the graduating class.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Even in a time-split state, the power of one shot was beyond Aider's capacity.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: No, it won't hit me. It can't hit me.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The deal with Fermi had certainly paid off.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: With Ghost Movement, he could graduate even without Fermi's gang's help.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? Even Shirone is nothing special? That's why you were just sucking your thumb while Maya got hit.
Shirone fired the Photon Cannon at maximum speed.
But Ghost Movement effortlessly pushed Aider's body aside, dodging the flash.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Hahaha! I told you, it won't work! So from the beginning...
At that moment, a powerful impact hit the back of his head.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Huh...?
Aider's leg wobbled.
The AT algorithm was broken. The fear of that fact was greater than the pain of the anti-magic.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Looking up in disbelief, Shirone's face blurred. The impact was much stronger than expected.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: It was a direct hit.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: But how?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The AT algorithm responds to all objects causing air resistance. Whether it came from behind or rose from the ground, it should have reacted.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: What... is that?

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Laser-guided algorithm.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: It was a high-level core comparable to the AT algorithm. The omnipotence of lasers itself was top-tier.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: That's why I...

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Dorothy's constructs also had functions similar to the AT algorithm. But the laser-guided algorithm was practically a nemesis to the automatic reflection function.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Damn it! Damn it! Damn it!
Aider flew around the peak, dodging the homing Photon Cannons. But no matter where he fled, he couldn't shake them off.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: He wanted to peel off the skin marked by the red dot.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: It was a spell with a power approaching 8,000 press per shot.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Damn it! What kind of spell is this!
The flight trajectory of the homing Photon Cannon, which intercepted a level 7 drone, was enough to catch up with the movements of a frontline professional magician.
Aider couldn't escape the clutches of the laser-guided algorithm and was eventually caught.
Ugh!
Just before colliding with the homing Photon Cannon, Aider's body wobbled, and the flash grazed past.
The relief was short-lived; true fear set in.
Eight beams of light slid endlessly over the AT algorithm like hyenas circling their prey.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Huh? Huh?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: As his body shook involuntarily, nausea surged. But the fear of the impending impact was even greater.
Aaaah!
The homing Photon Cannon, coiling around his waist like a snake, exploded on the back of his head.
Ugh!
If not for his endurance from passing survival level 4, he would have lost consciousness.
Aider, who had crashed to the ground, lifted his head. His emotions were laid bare, showing a demonic expression.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: This damn...!
Bang bang bang bang bang bang!
The successive impacts from the air drowned out Aider's voice.
The homing Photon Cannon showed no mercy. The unfeeling light bombarded wherever the laser guided.
Aaaah!
Aider clutched his head in agony. It felt like hundreds of spikes were drilling into his brain.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Realizing he couldn't win like this, he saw Shirone approaching and hurriedly knelt.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I-I'm sorry! Brother!
Shirone's steps halted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What did you do wrong?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Hitting Maya. Hiding my true nature and pretending otherwise. I'm originally like this. I can't help it. But I won't do it again. I'll apologize to her.
Magicians know exactly how their actions affect the outside world.
Therefore, they do not show anger unless it leads to the best outcome.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...Make sure to apologize to Maya.
Shirone walked past Aider.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Ah, Shirone... you really are...
Aider's eyes flickered as he watched Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: If he could land a proper blow on the back of Shirone's head, if he could see Shirone's eyes filled with blood and beat him...

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: How thrilling would that be?
The mere thought sent shivers down his spine, almost making him lose consciousness.
The anticipated pleasure led him into the Spirit Zone. With the AT algorithm activated, the Ghost Movement spell began to envelop him.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I can do it. At this distance, he can't counterattack!
Shirone looked up at the sky. His burning anger quickly froze over.
The sky was cold.
With 100% concentration, he sharpened his Spirit Zone, and the core and omnipotence created a powerful synergy.
Suppressing even the slightest anger, he added it to his magical power.
That was the anger of a magician.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Now!
Just as Aider, eyes wide open, was about to lunge at Shirone, Screamer shouted.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Idiot! Above! Above!
Aider's face went blank as he looked up.
Ataraxia was complete. And above it, the laser of the Homing Photon Cannon was aimed at his forehead.
It was different in power and scale from what he had used on Dante. The synchronization rate was 50% back then, but now it was 80%.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Damn it!
Aider clutched his head and crouched.
The Homing Photon Cannon pierced through Ataraxia, and a massive flash shot straight down from 10 meters above.
Aaaah!
The massive waterfall of energy turned into anti-magic, destroying Aider's mind. His nose bled like a cannon, and tears of blood flowed.
Rumble!
Aider's body began to vibrate at a terrifying speed.
While humans are not subject to the jungle rule, the interaction between spells was clearly implemented.
The AT algorithm, caught in the shockwave, began to function, moving Aider.
The problem was... there was nowhere to escape.
Ugh! Ugh!
Trapped in the massive flash, Aider bounced at an incredible speed. It was as if someone was shaking his spine at will.
Crack! Crack!
The sound of bones breaking echoed, shocking everyone.
The flash lasted only 1.6 seconds, but it was enough time to break every bone.
Aider, flung out of the flash, rolled down the hill. His eyes, staring at the sky, were devoid of pupils.
Shirone walked back to Aider after Ataraxia disappeared.
Even seeing Aider's limbs twisted in impossible directions, his eyes remained calm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Keep your promise to apologize to Maya.
If it had been Shirone who was broken, would Aider's actions have been justified?
Was it Maya's weakness, unfit for a magician? Or Aider's extreme cruelty, fitting for a magician?
Was this just part of the harsh competitive system of the graduating class?
There was no need to judge. Shirone would continue forward, and Aider's journey in the graduating class ended here.
For a magician, that was all that mattered.
The doors to the battlefield opened, and medics rushed in.
